description: A quantity is 'henoi' when it meets a relevant need. This is a judgement of sufficiency, not a claim that the amount is large: 'sheloi' (MANY) can be insufficient, and a small amount can be enough. The purpose or situation supplies the standard.
articulatory_notes: 'h' starts as open unvoiced breath and settles on unrounded front 'e'. For 'no', place your tongue tip at the upper front teeth, add nasal voice, and round the mouth for 'o'. Break cleanly before final high 'i'; stress the middle syllable.
sound_symbolism: An easy exhale begins the word, and contact at 'n' gives its middle a firm moment before the final vowel lifts away. The settling and lift offer a bodily image of reaching a threshold and stopping.
usage_notes: Before a noun, 'henoi' quantifies it directly, as in 'henoi phialu'. It can also predicate with 'nai' in 'phialu henoi nai'. When the quantity is understood, 'henoi.' makes a complete response on its own.

pillars.solarpunk-values: 'henoi' gives sufficiency its own positive word, so a design discussion can ask whether water or stored food meets a need without treating growth as the only success.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: In meditation, 'henoi' can name this breath or this session enough. The judgement stays with the present practice rather than with maximization.
